manager.worker-settings.empty: No worker CLI profiles are registered.
manager.worker-settings.help: Local CLI profiles on the server PC that Manager Assistant can match with implementation, verification, and recovery work. Check status before real runs to confirm installation and launch readiness.
manager.worker-settings.title: Worker CLI
settings.help.language: Language is stored in this browser. Korean is the default, and English can be selected from Settings > General.
settings.language.help: Choose the interface language for this browser. Korean is the default.
settings.language.title: Language
settings.language.unavailable: Unsupported saved languages are reset to Korean.
manager.orchestration.empty.artifacts: No artifact paths detected yet. Scan after worker output references files.
manager.orchestration.empty.decisions: No active project decisions recorded yet.
manager.orchestration.empty.health: No round health gate yet. Select or dispatch a round to verify worker evidence.
manager.orchestration.empty.manager-stat: No manager state is loaded yet.
manager.orchestration.empty.project-mana: No current manager state signal is linked to the selected project.
manager.orchestration.empty.protocol: Select a project to scan protocol files.
manager.orchestration.empty.protocol-sca: No protocol scan has loaded yet.
manager.orchestration.empty.flow: Select a project to write the command flow and start orchestration.
manager.orchestration.empty.worker-runs: No worker runs recorded for this round yet.
manager.orchestration.flow.warning.missi: Project charter goal is not recorded.
manager.orchestration.flow.warning.user-: A user verification blocker is open.
manager.orchestration.global.empty: No global manager signal is active.
manager.orchestration.global.title: Global manager status
manager.orchestration.hygiene.empty.proj: No project hygiene issues were found.
manager.orchestration.mode.admin-help: Shows operational tools such as state, run logs, decisions, and hygiene.
manager.orchestration.mode.user-help: Focuses on goal, progress, protocol, blockers, and artifacts.
manager.orchestration.next-action.dispat: Dispatch agents or ask the manager to plan the next round.
manager.orchestration.next-action.inspec: Inspect {role} and ask the manager for a recovery instruction.
Inspect {role} and decide whether to retry task {taskId}.
manager.orchestration.next-action.review: Review artifacts and ask the manager to summarize the round result.
Review failed task evidence before starting another round.
manager.orchestration.next-action.watch: Watch for fresh worker signals and artifact updates before closing the round.
manager.orchestration.current-judgment.h: Report is older than the current round
manager.orchestration.current-judgment.r: You can ask the manager a question or send a new instruction.
This project is completed. No further approval is needed.
Ask the manager to observe and summarize the current round again.
manager.orchestration.overview.no-round-: No round objective is available yet.
manager.orchestration.placeholder.blocke: What is blocked, what evidence exists, and what action is required?
manager.orchestration.placeholder.decisi: What was decided, and what should future agents remember?
manager.orchestration.recovery.acknowled: Keep the history but clear acknowledged failures from current state
manager.orchestration.title: Agent orchestration
manager.orchestration.toolbar.artifacts-: Derived from recent worker output until the project is scanned.
manager.orchestration.toolbar.blockers: Record only actionable blockers. Transient daemon/network noise should stay diagnostic until it needs a clear owner.
manager.orchestration.toolbar.decisions: Capture decisions that explain why the manager chose a protocol, role split, or recovery path.
manager.orchestration.agent.next.assign: Assign a task before the next round.
manager.orchestration.agent.next.inspect: Inspect task {taskId} and decide retry or direction change.
manager.orchestration.agent.next.manager: Manager review is needed before continuing.
manager.orchestration.agent.next.promote: Summarize the dry run, then choose real run or replan.
manager.orchestration.agent.next.review-: Review the output and turn it into the next round instruction.
manager.orchestration.agent.next.summari: Summarize this role before closing the round.
manager.orchestration.agent.next.wait: Wait for a fresh signal before changing direction.
manager.orchestration.agent.output.compl: Completed, but no output preview was captured.
manager.orchestration.agent.output.dry-r: Dry run completed; no live transcript payload was captured.
manager.orchestration.agent.output.needs: Needs inspection before the result can be trusted.
manager.orchestration.approval.default-u: Please confirm the current orchestration result before continuing.
manager.orchestration.approval.done: Approval action ran. State has been refreshed.
manager.orchestration.approval.error.com: This project is already completed. There is no approval action left to run.
manager.orchestration.approval.error.no-: No project was available for this approval.
This approval action is missing a round id.
manager.orchestration.approval.error.sta: This approval belongs to an older round. Refresh the current state and choose again.
manager.orchestration.approval.error.no-: This approval action is missing a task id.
manager.orchestration.approval.dismiss-e: Hides the "{action}" proposal and keeps watching the current state.
manager.orchestration.approval.title: Manager approval inbox
manager.orchestration.evidence.title: Evidence ledger
manager.orchestration.judgment.reason.ac: The round is already accepted. Start another round or complete the project.
manager.orchestration.judgment.reason.bl: A readiness condition needs manager attention before continuing.
manager.orchestration.judgment.reason.co: Confirm the completion criteria and approve final handling.
The loop can proceed to the next step.
manager.orchestration.judgment.reason.di: The next direction should be adjusted to match protocol or result signals.
manager.orchestration.judgment.reason.re: No blocking evidence was found, so the round is waiting for review approval.
Failed or missing worker evidence should be repaired first.
manager.orchestration.judgment.reason.us: A user checkpoint is open and needs direct confirmation.
manager.orchestration.judgment.reason.wa: Keep observing until active worker results arrive.
manager.orchestration.judgment.summary.a: Round accepted. Choose the next action.
manager.orchestration.proposed-action.ef: Records the completion criteria and moves the project to completed.
Records the new goal or constraint and uses it for later round decisions.
Opens the selected task log to inspect the failure cause and actual result.
Rechecks the project charter, protocol, and start requirements.
Repairs missing or tangled round references, then judges the current state again.
Opens a user checkpoint and asks for direct confirmation before continuing.
Runs the failed or missing task again to restore worker evidence.
Records the current round as accepted and moves to the next decision.
Reads protocol files again and refreshes the project ledger and diagram state.
Checks the next round plan and impact without real execution.
Starts the next live round from the accepted current result.
Keeps the state unchanged until new worker output or user input arrives.
manager.orchestration.project.label: Project
manager.orchestration.project.summary-no: No project is pinned yet. Current rounds are still visible, but they are not grouped by project.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.che: After the first round, review evidence, blockers, and direction changes with the human.
Before completion, the human confirms the final result or explicitly delegates acceptance.
Before orchestration starts, confirm the charter, protocol source, and read/write boundaries.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.del: Working app changes, verified build/test evidence, and a short handoff summary.
Runnable automation, trigger/config notes, rollback notes, and verification evidence.
Cleaned dataset or analysis output with source assumptions, checks, and reproducible steps.
Finished document artifact, review notes, and any unresolved source assumptions.
Targeted change set, regression evidence, and notes for remaining risks.
Playable game loop, asset/state notes, and interaction verification evidence.
Completed artifact, verification evidence, and a concise decision log.
Working website changes, responsive smoke evidence, and deployment or handoff notes.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.eve: The human explicitly applied wizard charter changes.
The human created this project through the intent wizard.
No manager signal will be emitted until the human changes the charter.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.gen: Plan with the manager, run design/implementation/feedback rounds, and record direction changes before the next round.
Keep API use and data read/write effects explicit; ask the human before irreversible or high-impact changes.
Do not expand beyond the stated intent without a manager decision and human confirmation.
Verify the {kind} with the relevant build, runtime, UI, or data checks, then record evidence before review.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.gui: Apply only meaningful direction changes; the manager will see the signal in the command flow before the next round proceeds.
After creation, review the charter in Flow, refresh readiness, then start orchestration when the human intent is correct.
Use this when the human has checked the result or explicitly delegated final acceptance.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.hel: Use an absolute path on the server PC so the project is created in the intended workspace.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.pla: Who will use or review it
Describe what the human wants to make, why it matters, and what must stay under their control.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.suc: The app supports the intended workflow, handles expected states, and passes relevant checks.
The automation runs safely, reports what it changed, and can be stopped or rolled back.
The output is reproducible, assumptions are stated, and the human can trust the checks.
The document is accurate, structured for the audience, and ready for human review.
The requested change works without regressing existing behavior.
The game loop is playable, responsive, and clear enough for the intended player.
The final result matches the intent and leaves clear evidence of what changed.
The site communicates the intended offer or workflow and works across target viewports.
manager.orchestration.project-wizard.war: Use an absolute path. Relative paths are interpreted from the server process and can create the project in the wrong place.
manager.worker-settings.profile.claude-c: Runs a separate Claude CLI process for implementation, verification, and repo work.
manager.worker-settings.profile.powershe: Runs server-local PowerShell commands for inspection, repair scripts, and maintenance.
manager.orchestration.protocol.diagram.i: Inspect worker output and touched files
manager.orchestration.protocol.diagram.n: No active protocol rules pinned yet
manager.orchestration.protocol.diagram.s: The current protocol is shown as the manager round loop, verification gate, and pinned rules.
manager.orchestration.protocol.diagram.v: Verify with filesystem and declared checks
manager.orchestration.confirm.clear-prot: Clear the latest protocol change record?
